frameworkName: Central Bank of the UAE — Information Assurance Regulation 2024
applicability: All banks, financial institutions, exchange houses, payment service providers, and insurance companies licensed and supervised by the Central Bank of the UAE

controls.title: Information Security Governance
controls.description: Licensed financial institutions must establish a formal information security governance structure with board-level accountability, an appointed CISO, and an information security policy aligned to CBUAE requirements.
requiredEvidence: Information security governance roles documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) citing CBUAE Information Assurance Regulation
CISO or security ownership referenced in project scope documentation
Information security policy framework referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Data Protection and Privacy
controls.description: Customer personal and financial data must be protected in accordance with UAE Federal Data Protection Law and CBUAE privacy requirements, including consent management and data subject rights.
requiredEvidence: Data protection requirements documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) referencing UAE Federal Data Protection Law
Customer consent management component described in SDS Component Specifications section
Privacy controls documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection

controls.title: Cyber Incident Response and Reporting
controls.description: Licensed institutions must maintain a documented cyber incident response plan and report significant cyber incidents to CBUAE within defined notification timelines.
requiredEvidence: Incident response plan with CBUAE notification timeline documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Security incident escalation path described in SDS Security Architecture section
Incident detection mechanism referenced in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Cryptography and Key Management
controls.description: Cryptographic controls must protect data at rest and in transit using CBUAE-approved algorithms, with formal key management procedures covering generation, storage, rotation, and destruction.
requiredEvidence: Encryption algorithm and key management policy documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection citing CBUAE requirements
Key storage and rotation procedures referenced in SDS Security Architecture section
API encryption requirements documented in SDS API Contracts section

controls.title: Access Control and Identity Management
controls.description: A comprehensive identity and access management framework must enforce role-based access control, multi-factor authentication, and privileged access management across all systems.
requiredEvidence: RBAC model and MFA requirements documented in SDS Security Architecture — Authentication and Authorization subsections
Privileged access management policy referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Identity lifecycle management described in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Network Security and Architecture
controls.description: Financial institution networks must implement segmentation, intrusion detection, and perimeter security controls to protect banking systems from unauthorised access and cyber threats.
requiredEvidence: Network segmentation architecture described in SDS Architecture Overview and Section 1.1 Architecture Diagram
Firewall and intrusion detection requirements documented in SDS Security Architecture section
Network security controls referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Third-Party and Cloud Risk Management
controls.description: All third-party service providers and cloud platforms used by licensed institutions must undergo formal risk assessment and ongoing monitoring per CBUAE outsourcing guidelines.
requiredEvidence: Third-party risk assessment requirements documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) citing CBUAE outsourcing guidelines
External vendors and cloud dependencies listed in SDS Integration Points section
Vendor security requirements referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Business Continuity and Operational Resilience
controls.description: Licensed institutions must maintain tested business continuity and disaster recovery plans ensuring operational resilience of critical banking services within CBUAE-defined recovery objectives.
requiredEvidence: RTO/RPO targets and availability SLA documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
High availability and failover architecture described in SDS Architecture Overview section
Business continuity strategy referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: AML/CFT Compliance Controls
controls.description: Financial systems must implement AML and counter-terrorism financing controls including customer due diligence, transaction monitoring, and suspicious activity reporting per UAE AML legislation.
requiredEvidence: AML/CFT requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) referencing UAE AML Law and CBUAE AML guidelines
KYC and CDD component described in SDS Component Specifications section
Suspicious transaction reporting integration documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Audit Trail and Activity Logging
controls.description: All financial transactions, privileged access events, and system configuration changes must be logged to an immutable audit trail retained for the regulatory minimum period.
requiredEvidence: Audit logging requirements with retention period documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Audit logging component described in SDS Security Architecture — Audit Logging subsection
Log aggregation or SIEM integration referenced in SDS Integration Points section
